zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Knapp於大約1515年出生在英國波克夏潘波恩時，她的父親Richard Knapp Sir13歲，母親Lady Margaret Francis11歲。她於1546年與Thomas Tisdale Sr.結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1555年之前。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Knapp于大约1515年出生在英国波克夏潘波恩时，她的父亲Richard Knapp Sir13岁，母亲Lady Margaret Francis11岁。她于1546年与Thomas Tisdale Sr.结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1555年之前。

nameMeanings.name: Knapp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: occupational or status name from the German word Knapp(e), a variant of Knabe ‘young unmarried man’. In the 15th century this spelling acquired the separate, specialized meanings ‘servant, apprentice’, and ‘miner’. This surname is also found elsewhere in central Europe, e.g. in Czechia and Slovakia, where it is more commonly spelled Knap (compare 3 below).
German: in Franconia, a nickname for a dexterous or skillful person, of the same ultimate origin as 1 above.
Germanized or Americanized form of Polish, Czech, Slovak, Rusyn, and Slovenian   Knap  , a surname of ultimately German origin (see 1 above).

variants.name: Knappe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Klaus, Rainer, Siegfried, Wolf.

variants.name: Knap
meanings: Polish, Czech, Slovak, Rusyn, and Slovenian: of German origin (see   Knapp   1), meaning e.g. ‘pageboy, armor bearer’ (Czech), ‘weaver’ (Polish, Slovak, and Rusyn), ‘wool comber’ (Slovak and Slovenian), and ‘miner’ (Slovenian and Czech).

variants.name: Knabe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erna, Gerhard, Helmut, Volker.

German: status name for a young man or a page, from Middle High German knabe. In aristocratic circles this term denoted a page or squire (a youth destined to become a knight), while among artisans it referred to a journeyman's assistant or (as a short form of Lehrknabe) ‘apprentice’. In the 15th century a semantic split between Knabe and its variants Knape, Knapp(e) resulted in Knabe meaning ‘boy’ and Knapp(e) ‘servant, apprentice’, and ‘miner’ in modern German.

variants.name: Canup
meanings: Americanized form of North German   Knapp   or   Knopp  .

variants.name: Knupp
meanings: German: variant of   Knapp   or   Knopp  .

variants.name: Knibbs
meanings: English: variant, with post-medieval excrescent -s, of Knibb, which may be related to Knapp or Nobbs or Knipe .

variants.name: Knapper
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English knap(p)er(e), an agent derivative of knap(pe) ‘knob, hillock’, for someone who lived by a hilltop or hillock; compare Knapp .
German (also Knäpper): habitational name for someone from either of two places in Westphalia named Knapp.
German (Knäpper): unflattering nickname from an agent derivative of knappen ‘to be stingy’ or, in some places, ‘to grab or snatch’.

variants.name: Knapke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Aloys, Florian.
German: from a pet form of   Knapp  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Knapp was born about 1515, in Pangbourne, Berkshire, England, her father, Richard Knapp Sir, was 13 and her mother, Lady Margaret Francis, was 11. She married Thomas Tisdale Sr. in 1546. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died before 1555.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Knapp est née environ 1515, à Pangbourne, Berkshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard Knapp Sir, avait 13 ans et sa mère, Lady Margaret Francis, avait 11 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Tisdale Sr. en 1546. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée avant 1555.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Knapp um 1515 in Pangbourne, Berkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard Knapp Sir 13 und ihre Mutter Lady Margaret Francis 11. Sie hat 1546 Thomas Tisdale Sr. geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb vor 1555.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Knapp è nata il circa 1515 nel Pangbourne, Berkshire, England, sua padre, Richard Knapp Sir, aveva 13 anni e sua madre, Lady Margaret Francis, aveva 11 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Tisdale Sr. nel 1546. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì prima del 1555.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Knapp(이)가 대략 1515년 영국 버크실르 파그보우른에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Knapp Sir(은)는 13세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Margaret Francis(은)는 11세였다. 그녀는 1546년 Thomas Tisdale Sr.(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1555년 이전 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Knapp родилась около 1515 г. в местности пангбурн, биркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Knapp Sir было 13 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Margaret Francis, 11. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Tisdale Sr. в 1546 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла перед 1555 г.
